title: MATRIYA Failure Case
description: A structured industrial FAILURE (or near-failure) case — not a document, a failure object. It captures why a formulation/system failed, links it to the Material Behavior Model, and records negative knowledge (conditions under which it must not operate). Cases are provenance-bound; illustrative pilot cases are flagged as such. No invented chemistry: only standard, documented failure modes.

outcome.description: near_failure = the Near-Failure Library: it almost failed. The 'almost' is where new mechanisms hide.

margin.description: for near_failure: how close to the edge (e.g. 'adhesion dropped to 0.8 MPa, spec 0.7').

conditions.description: the driver/environment envelope in which the failure occurred.

cascade.description: ordered event sequence — the Failure Pattern Engine mines recurring sub-sequences across cases.

mappedMBM.description: link into the Material Behavior Model. States/transitions the MBM lacks are how this corpus generates ΔK. Novelty is DERIVED by the pilot against the live MBM, not declared here.

competingMechanismFor.description: id of an existing MBM transition this case offers an ALTERNATIVE explanation for.

brokenInvariant.description: which world-model invariant the failure appears to challenge (a candidate boundary/law condition).

antiConditions.description: Negative Knowledge: conditions under which this system must NOT operate (FSCTM Boundary). The knowledge of where it fails.

source.description: where this came from; for pilot cases a domain-standard reference class, not a fabricated citation.

flag.description: illustrative = standard/textbook failure mode used for the pilot; sourced = a specific provenance-bound document (ingestion era).
